reasoning: The question asks if the NASA GISTEMP global temperature anomaly for October 2025 will be below 1.10°C (110 in the dataset). Recent data shows global temperatures running extremely high; September 2025 was the 3rd warmest September on record, barely cooler than 2024. For September 2023 to be the hottest (anomaly ~1.44°C) and September 2025 to be 3rd hottest, its anomaly must be well above 1.10°C (likely around 1.25–1.35°C). While a La Niña pattern could cool temperatures compared to the 2023/2024 El Niño peaks, a sudden drop from >1.25°C in September to <1.10°C in October is climatologically unlikely. Thus, it's highly probable the October 2025 figure will remain at or above 1.10°C.
